beats.text: You found it. Good. I have been waiting to see what the Lottery would send me.
Sit down. We have work to do before I leave, and not much time to do it in.

topics.prompt: What do you need to teach me?

opening_beats.text: What you already know shapes what I can teach you quickly. Don't tell me what you think I want to hear. Tell me what you have actually done with your hands.

responses.text: I've spent most of my time working with materials — alchemy, craft.

beats.text: Then I can give you what the materials cannot teach you alone: how to read what is happening underneath the reaction, not just on the surface. Alchemical Meditation. It will come naturally to someone already practiced.

responses.text: Survival, mostly. Moving through difficult terrain, finding what I need.

beats.text: Useful. And honest. Alchemical thinking will expand what you already know about reading an environment. Start there — it will translate.

responses.text: I'm not sure how to describe it. A little of everything.

beats.text: Generalists are harder to teach and harder to kill. I can work with that. Alchemical Meditation first — it gives structure to instinct.

opening_beats.text: It has done what I needed it to do. I no longer need it where I am going. You might.

beats.text: Somewhere I can only reach alone. You will understand later. For now — listen to Chancellor Kelly. And Duke Eomann when he speaks. They know things that will matter before this is over.
Not soon. But I know where you will be. Pay attention to Chancellor Kelly and Duke Eomann — they are not what they appear to their peers. That distinction will matter.

opening_beats.text: Then go. Everything I can give you in this room, I have given you.
